_meta.description: Koinkat Enable Banking mock fixtures - 3 months, 3 accounts
_meta.generated_for: Marco Rossi - freelance UX designer, Milan/Copenhagen

split_notes.SPLIT_DINNER_1_PARENT: Feb dinner €120, 3 friends owe €30 each. 2 repaid (REPAY_1, REPAY_2). Sofia owes €30 → split open.
split_notes.SPLIT_AIRBNB_PARENT: Mar Airbnb €210 for 3 people. Lorenzo repaid €70 (REPAY_1). Emma owes €70 → split open.

recurring_notes.clean_monthly: SPOTIFY AB (€10.99), VODAFONE ITALIA (€29.99) and NETFLIX repeat on the same day each month. Flag one in Review (month 1) → the next month's charge is recognized SILENTLY and skips the Review queue.
recurring_notes.amount_change: NETFLIX INTERNATIONAL BV is €13.99 in Jan/Feb but €17.99 in Mar (a ~29% jump). After flagging it, the March charge attaches but surfaces a 'still the same plan?' confirmation in Review.
recurring_notes.irregular_groceries: COOP ITALIA SPA recurs at irregular gaps and amounts (groceries). It must NEVER be auto-flagged as recurring - no stable cadence, so the matcher leaves it alone unless the user explicitly flags it.
recurring_notes.missed_charge: The fixtures end in March; with a later 'today' any flagged active series whose next expected charge never arrived surfaces as a quiet 'usually hits around …' notice on Budgets/Dashboard - never a Review row.

transaction_lifecycle._doc: Scripted pending->book->vanish demo for uid-fineco-eur-lifecycle. The mock advances `rounds` by one per sync (on the booked fetch). Linking the Fineco bank runs round 0 automatically; each subsequent manual Sync advances one round. Dates use *_day_offset (days before today) resolved against the real clock. Round 0: charge A appears pending. Round 1: A books (same row flips) + B and C appear pending. Round 2: B vanishes without booking (auto-removed) while C books.
